grammar.name: ✅ Finite Indicative Active Past Simple First Singular
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Express the action plainly without specifying duration or extent over time: 'I run', 'I choose', 'I am strong.'
- Use the past tense, e.g., 'I was...', 'I chose...'.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I was cautious before making a bold decision
I chose wisdom over rash action
I found my strength in knowing the cost of a move

grammar.name: ⭐ Finite Indicative Active Present Simple First Singular
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Express the action plainly without specifying duration or extent over time: 'I run', 'I choose', 'I am strong.'
- Use the present tense, e.g., 'I am...', 'I trust...'.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I choose wisely before I commit to a path
I trust that wisdom guides my decisions
I am prepared to act with foresight

grammar.name: ⏭️ Finite Indicative Active Future Simple First Singular
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Express the action plainly without specifying duration or extent over time: 'I run', 'I choose', 'I am strong.'
- Use the future tense with 'will': 'I will be strong', 'I will achieve...', 'I will find...'
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I will find peace by knowing when to pause before committing to a path
I will choose wisely between options by weighing the cost against the benefit
I will find strength by understanding the full picture before taking action

examples: I have been building my resilience.
I have been growing every day.
grammar.name: 🔄 Finite Indicative Active Present Perfect Progressive First Singular
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Use the perfect progressive: 'I have been [verb]ing...'. The sentence must open with 'I have been'. Coordinated verbs must also use the -ing form, e.g., 'I have been growing and becoming...'.
- Use the present tense, e.g., 'I am...', 'I trust...'.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I have been observing the path with a calm vigilance
I have been weighing the cost of every move before committing
I have been learning to see the bigger picture of the situation

examples: I will have overcome this challenge.
I will have become the person I am meant to be.
I will have created a life I love.
grammar.name: 🎯 Finite Indicative Active Future Perfect Perfective First Singular
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Express the action as a complete, bounded whole — viewed start-to-finish: 'I ran (and finished)', 'I achieved my goal', 'I overcame it.'
- Use FUTURE PERFECT tense: 'I will have [past participle]...'. This is DIFFERENT from present perfect ('I have [past participle]'). 'I will have become...', 'I will have achieved...', 'I will have created...' are all valid future perfect. The 'will have' structure is mandatory.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I will have seen the path clearly before stepping forward
I will have chosen the course of action that leads to lasting peace
I will have built a foundation of wisdom that guides me through every challenge

grammar.name: 🤝 Finite Indicative Active Present Simple First Plural
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Express the action plainly without specifying duration or extent over time: 'I run', 'I choose', 'I am strong.'
- Use the present tense, e.g., 'I am...', 'I trust...'.
- Subject is 'we' (first person plural).

affirmations.text: We are wise enough to pause before making a bold decision
We are grounded enough to understand the cost of our choices
We are balanced enough to see the true benefit of every move

examples: They believe in their own power.
grammar.name: 👤 Finite Indicative Active Present Simple Third
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Express the action plainly without specifying duration or extent over time: 'I run', 'I choose', 'I am strong.'
- Use the present tense, e.g., 'I am...', 'I trust...'.
- CRITICAL: Do NOT use 'I'. The subject must be third-person: 'he', 'she', 'it', or 'they'. Never start the sentence with 'I'.

affirmations.text: They are wise enough to recognize the cost of a move before committing to a path
She remains grounded in the balance between immediate action and long-term wisdom
They understand that true strength lies in knowing the full picture before acting

examples: I can handle whatever comes my way.
I could become who I dream of being.
grammar.name: 💪 Finite Potential Active Present Simple First Singular
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- CRITICAL: The main verb structure MUST use 'can' or 'could' or 'able to': 'I can...', 'I could...', 'I am able to...'. Do NOT use indicative verbs like 'trust', 'believe', or 'know' without 'can'/'could'.
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Express the action plainly without specifying duration or extent over time: 'I run', 'I choose', 'I am strong.'
- Use the present tense, e.g., 'I am...', 'I trust...'.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I can choose wisely before committing to any new path
I am able to see the cost and benefit of every decision
I could trust in the wisdom of knowing when to pause

examples: May I find clarity in this moment.
If only I could feel at peace.
Would that I might know my own worth.
grammar.name: 🙏 Finite Optative Active First Singular
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- Express a wish or hope, e.g., 'May I find...', 'If only I could...'.
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: May I find peace in the quiet of the shadows
If only I could see the path ahead with clarity
Would that I might know the cost of my choices

grammar.name: ❗ Finite Imperative Active Second Singular
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- Use the imperative mood: start the sentence with a bare infinitive verb — NO explicit subject. The implicit (unstated) subject is always 'you'. Examples: 'Be bold!', 'Trust yourself!', 'Embrace your strength!'. Subordinate clauses in the same sentence may use any voice, tense, or person — only the MAIN verb (the one that starts the sentence) must be bare imperative. Do NOT write 'You should...', 'You must...', or any sentence that starts with 'I'.
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Subject/address is 'you' (second person).

affirmations.text: Be wise to pause before committing to a path
Trust your judgment to choose wisely
Embrace the balance of action and wisdom

examples: Am I not capable of this?
Have I not already proven my resilience?
Can I not rise to this challenge?
grammar.name: ❓ Finite Interrogative Active Present First Singular Negative
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- CRITICAL: The sentence MUST be a QUESTION and MUST end with '?'. Required patterns: 'Am I not...?', 'Have I not...?', 'Can I not...?', 'Do I not...?'. The '?' at the very end of the sentence is MANDATORY — never omit it. Do NOT write a declarative statement. 'Can' and 'have' are valid finite auxiliaries.
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Include negation using 'not', 'never', 'no longer', or 'without'.
- Use the present tense, e.g., 'I am...', 'I trust...'.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: Am I not ready to face the storm that awaits me?
Have I not already learned the cost of rushing?
Can I not trust that wisdom guides me through the dark?

grammar.name: ♾️ Infinitive Active Simple
grammar.description:
- Start with the base infinitive: 'To [verb]...' — no subject or conjugation.
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Express the action plainly without specifying duration or extent over time: 'I run', 'I choose', 'I am strong.'

affirmations.text: To choose wisely before the path is paved
To act with the wisdom of knowing the cost
To remain grounded in the balance of action and observation

grammar.name: 💡 Gerund Active
grammar.description:
- Begin with a gerund (-ing form as a noun), e.g., 'Being brave is...'.
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.

affirmations.text: Being strategic is my strength
Choosing balance is my strength
Understanding wisdom is my strength

examples: I am supported by those around me.
grammar.name: 🌊 Finite Indicative Passive Present Simple First Singular
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- CRITICAL: The MAIN clause must use PASSIVE voice — the subject receives the action. Pattern: 'I am/was/have been [past participle]', e.g., 'I am loved', 'I am guided', 'I am supported by...'. Subordinate clauses and infinitive phrases (e.g., 'ready to feel', 'able to see') may use active voice. Do NOT write active main clauses like 'I trust', 'I choose', or 'I feel'.
- Express the action plainly without specifying duration or extent over time: 'I run', 'I choose', 'I am strong.'
- Use the present tense, e.g., 'I am...', 'I trust...'.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I am guided by the wisdom of the Six of Swords
I am supported by the balance of action and observation
I am calm and ready to see the bigger picture

examples: I consistently show up for myself.
grammar.name: 🔁 Finite Indicative Active Present Habitual First Singular
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Express a recurring action using 'always', 'regularly', 'consistently', or 'every day'.
- Use the present tense, e.g., 'I am...', 'I trust...'.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I always choose wisdom over rash decisions
I regularly observe the cost of every move I make
I consistently seek balance before taking action

examples: I am not defined by my past.
I am not afraid of change.
I do not limit my own potential.
grammar.name: 🫷 Finite Indicative Active Present Simple First Singular Negative
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Include negation using 'not', 'never', 'no longer', or 'without'.
- Express the action plainly without specifying duration or extent over time: 'I run', 'I choose', 'I am strong.'
- Use the present tense, e.g., 'I am...', 'I trust...'.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I am not defined by the path I choose today
I am not afraid of the cost I may incur now
I am not limited by the path I walk forward
